reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
Sunday at the end of May. The month is closing out, summer is leaning in, and there's nothing particularly remarkable about today. That used to be a problem for me. An unremarkable Sunday at 22 was something to be fixed — with a hangover-cure beer, a pre-game, a plan to make the day feel like something. Anything to avoid just being in it.

What I didn't understand then is that the unremarkable days are the whole thing. They're where the life actually happens. The Sunday morning coffee that doesn't need to be earned back from the night before. The afternoon walk with no agenda. The text from a friend you actually answer because you're present enough to hear your phone buzz. Durant's line about habit gets quoted to death, but it's true: you don't become someone you can live with in a single dramatic moment. You become that person in how you handle a quiet Sunday in May.

Eleven years in, I can tell you the dramatic stuff — the ski trips, the skydives, the long flights to places I never thought I'd see — none of it would exist without the ordinary days that built the foundation. Showing up sober for a Sunday you don't feel like showing up for is the work. The rest is what the work makes possible.
reflection.meditation: Sit with this day exactly as it is. Not the day you wish it were, not the day you're trying to make it into. Just this Sunday. Notice that you're here for it.

affirmations: I am building a life one ordinary day at a time, and today counts.
Today I show up for the small things, because the small things are the life.
I have the agency to choose how I meet this day, whatever it brings.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a quiet Sunday I can actually be present for. Grateful for the version of me that kept showing up long enough to get here, and for the people who showed up alongside him. The fact that an ordinary day feels like enough is something I never want to take for granted — there was a long time it didn't.
gratitude.closing: One more day. That's the whole gift.
